mnemonics: FILO (First In, Last Out) - Like a stack of plates: the first plate you put down is the last one you pick up.
keyPoints: A stack is a linear data structure that follows the LIFO (Last In, First Out) principle.
The stack has two primary operations: push (to add an element to the top) and pop (to remove the top element).
A pointer (often called 'top') is used to keep track of the index of the topmost element in the array.
commonMistakes: Attempting to pop an element from an empty stack (stack underflow).
Pushing an element onto a stack that has reached its maximum capacity (stack overflow).
